Hypothetical path (proposed, in order) from the provided start page to the target page. Each entry includes the page title, full URL, why I would choose that link on the prior page, and how the topics connect. NOTE: This path is plausible but unverified in the absence of visit evidence.

1. Artificial intelligence
   - URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence
   - Why choose this link: This is the starting page provided by the mandate.
   - Topic connection: Artificial intelligence (AI) is the broad field that includes subfields and intersections (such as machine learning and applications of computing technologies) which can lead to explorations of computational models and emerging computational paradigms.

2. Machine learning
   - URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Machine_learning
   - Why choose this link from page 1: The AI page commonly links to its major subfields; machine learning is the core subfield of AI and is typically linked directly from the AI page.
   - Topic connection: Machine learning is the dominant approach within AI today; exploring ML narrows the focus from general AI to algorithmic methods that often depend on computational models and resources — a natural stepping stone toward specialized intersections such as quantum machine learning.

3. Quantum machine learning
   - URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quantum_machine_learning
   - Why choose this link from page 2: The Machine learning page commonly discusses variants and emerging areas including quantum machine learning; the QM-learning page sits at the intersection of ML and quantum computing.
   - Topic connection: Quantum machine learning explicitly connects machine learning methods with quantum computing hardware and algorithms. It is the direct conceptual bridge from classical ML/AI topics to quantum computing research and methods.

4. Quantum computing (TARGET)
   - URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quantum_computing
   - Why choose this link from page 3: The Quantum machine learning page typically links to the main Quantum computing page to explain or reference the quantum-computing foundations that QM-learning uses.
   - Topic connection: Quantum computing is the foundational field providing the models, hardware, and algorithms (quantum bits, gates, algorithms like Shor’s and Grover’s) that enable quantum machine learning. This is the target page mandated by your task.

Required verification step (what I could produce if visit results were provided):
- For each step i in the chain above, supply the visit result for URL[i] showing that the page contains an explicit link to URL[i+1]. Then I will list each page visited in order, quote or cite the exact link text and location on the page, and explicitly state "URL[i] contains link to URL[i+1]" for adjacency verification.

References (given/provided by you):
1) Start page (given): https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence
2) Target page (given): https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quantum_computing

If you want me to actually verify the chain, please provide the page visit results (or allow me to fetch/visit them). With those visit outputs I will produce a fully verified, evidence-backed path listing each page visited in order, the exact link clicked on each page, and explicit adjacency statements (URL[i] contains link to URL[i+1]).
